gloss.en: a soft, round, purple or red fruit

words.example: She picked a ripe plum from the tree.

gloss.en: a person who fixes water pipes

words.example: We called a plumber to fix the leaking pipe.

gloss.en: used to show the real amount is more

words.example: The work will cost £10 000 plus.

gloss.en: a serious illness of the lungs

words.example: He was in hospital with pneumonia.

gloss.en: having a sharp end

words.example: He has a pointed chin.

gloss.en: having no purpose or use

words.example: It is pointless to argue with him.

gloss.en: a serious disease that can cause paralysis
gloss.zh: 小儿麻痹症，脊髓灰质炎

words.example: A vaccine protects children from polio.

gloss.en: a substance used to make things shiny

words.example: She put some polish on her shoes.

gloss.en: behaviour that is respectful and well-mannered

words.example: He thanked them out of politeness.

gloss.en: in a way connected with politics

words.example: The country is politically stable.

gloss.en: a society as an organized political group

words.example: They built a strong and lasting polity.

gloss.en: a ball game played on horseback

words.example: They watched a game of polo.

gloss.en: to think carefully about something

words.example: She paused to ponder the question.

gloss.en: to make something liked by many people

words.example: The show helped popularise the sport.

gloss.en: the state of being liked by many people

words.example: The increasing popularity of cycling is clear.

gloss.en: to make something liked by many people

words.example: The book helped popularize the idea.

gloss.en: a covered area at the door of a house

words.example: We sat on the porch all evening.

gloss.en: one part of something larger

words.example: A large portion of the population is poor.

gloss.en: a position you hold for a photo or painting

words.example: She held a graceful pose for the camera.

gloss.en: expensive and looking upper-class

words.example: They had dinner at a posh restaurant.

gloss.en: the place where someone or something is

words.example: Where is the best position for the lights?

gloss.en: good or useful

words.example: The tests have yielded positive results.

gloss.en: to have or own something

words.example: He does not possess a car.

gloss.en: controlled by a strong feeling or spirit

words.example: He fought like a man possessed.

gloss.en: connected with sending mail

words.example: Please write your postal address here.

gloss.en: very strong or powerful

words.example: This is a very potent drug.

gloss.en: that can develop in the future

words.example: They are talking to potential customers.

gloss.en: in a way that may develop into something

words.example: It is a potentially dangerous situation.

gloss.en: the ability to control people or things

words.example: He has the power to change the rules.

gloss.en: in a strong and forceful way

words.example: The story affected me powerfully.

gloss.en: how useful or sensible something is

words.example: We must consider the practicality of the plan.

gloss.en: almost; in a practical way

words.example: The room was practically empty.

gloss.en: words that show you admire someone

words.example: The coach singled out two players for praise.

gloss.en: to move with lively, proud steps

words.example: The horse began to prance around the field.

gloss.en: a small shellfish that you can eat

words.example: We ordered a plate of fried prawn.

gloss.en: to happen or come before something

words.example: A loud noise will precede the storm.

gloss.en: rare and worth a lot of money

words.example: She kept a precious vase on the shelf.

gloss.en: clear and accurate

words.example: He gave us precise instructions.

gloss.en: exactly

words.example: They look precisely the same to me.

gloss.en: a difficult or unpleasant situation

words.example: I was in a real predicament.

gloss.en: to base something on a particular thing

words.example: Their success was predicate on hard work.

gloss.en: coming after a verb like 'be'

words.example: In this sentence the adjective is predicative.

gloss.en: known in advance; not surprising

words.example: The ending was completely predictable.

gloss.en: able to show what will happen

words.example: The app uses predictive text.

gloss.en: a thing that shows what will happen

words.example: Age is a good predictor of health.

gloss.en: an older student with some authority

words.example: She was made a prefect at school.

gloss.en: better or more suitable

words.example: A quiet job is preferable to a noisy one.

gloss.en: used to say what you would choose

words.example: Call me soon, preferably before noon.

gloss.en: a greater liking for one thing

words.example: It is a matter of personal preference.

gloss.en: having a baby growing inside the body

words.example: My wife is pregnant.

gloss.en: from the time before written history

words.example: They found prehistoric cave paintings.

gloss.en: from before humans existed

gloss.en: from before factories and industry

words.example: Life was hard in preindustrial times.

gloss.en: to judge before knowing the facts

words.example: Try not to prejudge the situation.

gloss.en: to fill someone's mind completely

words.example: Money worries seem to preoccupy him.

gloss.en: the act of getting ready for something

words.example: Food preparation takes a long time.

gloss.en: the fact of being in a place

words.example: He hardly seemed to notice my presence.

gloss.en: a person who introduces a TV or radio show

words.example: She is a well-known TV presenter.

gloss.en: to keep something safe or unchanged

words.example: He was anxious to preserve his reputation.

gloss.en: respect and admiration from others

words.example: The job brings a lot of prestige.

gloss.en: to act as if something untrue is true

words.example: I am tired of having to pretend all the time.

gloss.en: meant to stop something bad happening

gloss.en: at a time before now

gloss.en: the amount of money you pay for something

words.example: The price of the boat is £8 000.

gloss.en: too valuable to have a price

words.example: The museum holds priceless paintings.

gloss.en: to make a small hole with a sharp point

words.example: Be careful not to prick your finger.

gloss.en: a feeling of pleasure in an achievement

words.example: Her son's success filled her with pride.

gloss.en: mainly

words.example: This course is designed primarily for experts.

gloss.en: main; or for young school children

words.example: She works as a primary teacher.

gloss.en: main; most important

words.example: My prime concern is to protect my home.

gloss.en: a moral rule or strong belief

gloss.en: letters and words printed on paper

words.example: The tiny print was hard to read.

gloss.en: happening before something else

words.example: Some prior knowledge of statistics is useful.

gloss.en: something more important to deal with first

words.example: Safety is a high priority for us.

gloss.en: being alone and not watched by others

words.example: She was longing for some peace and privacy.

gloss.en: in a private way; not in public

words.example: He told me privately what had happened.

gloss.en: how likely something is to happen

words.example: The probability is that prices will rise.

gloss.en: likely to happen or be true

words.example: What is the probable cause of the fire?

gloss.en: the usual or correct way of doing something

words.example: We must follow the safety procedure.

gloss.en: to continue doing something

words.example: We are not sure whether to proceed with the sale.

gloss.en: a series of steps to reach a result

words.example: The planning process took many months.

gloss.en: to make or grow something

words.example: These farms produce a lot of fruit.

gloss.en: done as a paid job, not a hobby

words.example: They are professional athletes.

gloss.en: a short description giving useful facts

words.example: We build a detailed profile of our customers.

gloss.en: money you make after paying costs

words.example: The company made a large profit this year.

gloss.en: a person who writes computer programs

words.example: He works as a computer programer.

words.example: She is learning computer programing.

words.example: My brother is a software programmer.

gloss.en: the work of writing computer programs

words.example: He is studying programming languages.

gloss.en: the process of improving or developing

words.example: I think we are making good progress.

gloss.en: in favour of new ideas and change

words.example: They send their kids to progressive schools.

gloss.en: to stop something, especially by law

words.example: A law prohibits the sale of alcohol here.

gloss.en: a planned piece of work or study

words.example: We are working on a history project.

gloss.en: a formal school dance party

words.example: She wore a new dress to the prom.

gloss.en: the state of being important or well known

words.example: The story rose to prominence in the news.

gloss.en: showing signs of being good or successful

words.example: He is a promising young actor.

gloss.en: a move to a more important job

words.example: The new job is a promotion for him.

gloss.en: to cause something to happen

words.example: The bomb threat will prompt more security.

gloss.en: quickly, without delay

words.example: She answered the email promptly.

gloss.en: information showing that something is true

words.example: They found scientific proof of the theory.

gloss.en: a person who supports an idea

words.example: She is a strong proponent of reform.

gloss.en: a part or share of a whole

words.example: Water covers a large proportion of the earth.

gloss.en: a formal suggestion or plan

gloss.en: to suggest a plan or idea

words.example: The government proposed changes to the system.

gloss.en: suggested as a plan or idea

words.example: We discussed the proposed changes.

gloss.en: ordinary written language, not poetry

words.example: She writes clear and simple prose.

gloss.en: the possibility that something will happen

words.example: There is no immediate prospect of peace.

gloss.en: to become successful or wealthy

words.example: The business began to prosper quickly.

gloss.en: the act of keeping someone or something safe

words.example: These clothes give protection against the rain.

gloss.en: a substance in food that helps you grow

words.example: Meat and eggs are rich in protein.

gloss.en: a person who joins a public protest

words.example: Thousands of protesters marched through the city.

gloss.en: in a way that shows pride

words.example: She proudly showed us her new car.

gloss.en: shown to be true or effective

gloss.en: the state of being near something

words.example: I like the house for its proximity to school.

gloss.en: connected with the mind and how it works
